channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Micron stock jumps over 16% in premarket trading after blockbuster earnings
item.description: The company's revenue more than quadrupled from $9.3 billion a year earlier to $41.46 billion, it reported on Wednesday

item.title: Chevron CFO says gas prices will normalize after Trump presses Big Oil
item.description: Her comments come shortly after President Donald Trump ordered an investigation into Big Oil, accusing them of "gouging" consumers.

item.title: Iran warns ships it's 'unacceptable and dangerous' to transit the Strait of Hormuz without their approval
item.description: The stern warning underscores Tehran's resolve to retain control over the Strait of Hormuz and to resist transits that bypass its authorization.

item.title: Trump pledges rapid U.S. response for Venezuela after historic earthquakes kill dozens
item.description: The swift U.S. offer of assistance reflects a degree of diplomatic realignment between the Trump administration and the Venezuelan interim government.

item.title: Fundstrat's Lee joins Wall Street bulls in calling for S&P at 8,000
item.description: Fundstrat Global Advisors' Tom Lee has joined a growing chorus of Wall Street bulls forecasting the S&P 500 will end the year at 8,000.

item.title: Oil prices erase wartime gains as supply concerns ease with Hormuz tanker traffic resuming
item.description: Oil prices fell as investors bet global crude supplies will improve, as tankers stranded in the Persian Gulf for months have begun leaving the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Defense stocks slide again, as Germany’s naval U-turn shakes confidence in Europe’s rearmament boom
item.description: Rheinmetall and other European defense stocks fell for a second day, after Germany scrapped the F126 naval program, raising procurement concerns.

item.title: Gold holds below $4,000, silver below $60 — has the shimmer worn off the precious metal rally?
item.description: Gold is down almost 8% so far this year, while silver has shed more than 20%.

item.title: SK Hynix surges 12% after Micron earnings; blockbuster Nasdaq listing
item.description: Shares of South Korean chip giant SK Hynix surged 12% on Thursday after the company said it is seeking to raise as much as $29.4 billion.

item.title: Federal Reserve says U.S. banks can withstand $708 billion in losses amid overhaul of capital rules
item.description: The Fed's annual exercise comes at a pivotal moment for bank regulation because, unlike previous years, the results will not affect capital requirements.

item.title: Iran's neighbor, U.S. ally: What Pakistan gains from being a peacemaker
item.description: Pakistan's efforts to bring an end to the conflict in the Middle East are driven by the need to avoid a spillover across its borders.

item.title: Anthropic's latest hiring spree reveals where it's building AI data centers next
item.description: The AI lab is hiring for AI data center roles in Australia and Japan as it rushes to expand compute capacity overseas.

item.title: Amazon adds new funding, lifting India AI and cloud investment to $48 billion
item.description: Amazon announces fresh investment of $13 billion to expand AI and cloud infrastructure in India, taking its total investment to $48 billion.

item.title: Qualcomm stock pops 15% after chipmaker almost doubles projection for 2029 non-handset revenue
item.description: Qualcomm's primary business has been smartphones, which accounted for two-thirds of the company's product revenue in it's most recent quarter.

item.title: White House requests $87.6 billion supplemental spending for Iran war, farm aid
item.description: White House Office of Management and Budget Director Russell Vought asked House Speaker Mike Johnson for billions in extra spending for the Iran war.

item.title: Federal student loans have a new interest rate discount — here's who qualifies
item.description: To qualify for the U.S. Department of Education's new interest rate discount on student loans, borrowers must meet several requirements. Here's what to know.

item.title: Despite Trump trade rhetoric, Asian investors keep betting on U.S.: Former House Majority Leader Eric Cantor
item.description: Cantor said that investment flows still have "so much" allocation to the U.S., because investors recognise that the country was still one with a "rule of law."

item.title: These are Schwab's top three income ideas for the rest of 2026
item.description: Collin Martin of Schwab Center for Financial Research lays out where he sees the best opportunity for income investors right now.

item.title: Inside India newsletter: Meet the humans teaching robots to perform routine tasks, as India finds a way to enter the AI race
item.description: Several companies have cropped up in India providing video training data made by humans that is being used to teach robots in the U.S. and China.

item.title: This industrial play has been a quiet winner in 2026. Why the stock has more room to go
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Trump meets defense CEOs as Iran operations strain U.S. missile stockpiles
item.description: The White House is pressing major defense firms to expand missile and munitions output as Iran talks and weapons stockpile concerns put new pressure on the Pentagon’s industrial base.

item.title: How much does debt settlement cost?
item.description: A debt settlement company could substantially lower your credit card bills. Find out what (and how) they charge for their services.

item.title: One of India's largest gold exporters paid its managing director just $180 a month, investigators say in accounting probe
item.description: Indian authorities have found accounting discrepancies in books of Indian gold company Rajesh Exports, and owner of Swiss refiner Valcambi.

item.title: South Korea’s IPO bust clouds equity markets as Chaebol structure restrains listings
item.description: South Korea's IPO activity has lagged that of its regional peers as the structure of the market and governance reforms collide.

item.title: Investors still seek a human touch even with AI tools at hand: HSBC
item.description: Investors are still turning to the judgment of a professional adviser in their last investment decision, despite using AI tools, a HSBC survey found.

item.title: Trump cancels signing of bipartisan housing bill ahead of tense meeting with GOP senators
item.description: President Donald Trump abruptly canceled the signing of a bill aimed at increasing housing affordability an hour before he was due at the Capitol for the ceremony.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of campaign to 'brazenly' and 'illicitly' extract AI capabilities
item.description: The letter, which was obtained by CNBC, claims Alibaba carried out "the largest known distillation attack on Anthropic to date."

item.title: JPMorgan Chase unveils $50 billion buyback, Goldman Sachs raises dividend after Fed stress test
item.description: The announcements followed the release of the Federal Reserve's annual stress test, which found that all 32 large banks weathered a hypothetical recession.

item.title: Energy secretary says U.S. has ended Iran's ability to close Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Some 72 ships loaded with 19 million barrels of oil have passed through Hormuz in the past 24 hours, Energy Secretary Chris Wright said.

item.title: Identity theft victims face 'unconscionable' IRS delays, report says
item.description: Victims currently face wait times of almost two years to resolve their cases, according to the National Taxpayer Advocate.
